result:
I have completed the validation of the knowledge file `libraries-tag_reference` against its source.

## Summary

**Status:** has_issues (12 minor findings)

**Critical Issues:** None found
- ✅ No information omissions - all substantive content preserved
- ✅ No fabricated content - all text traceable to source
- ✅ Navigation lists acceptably omitted per policy

**Minor Issues:** Hints incompleteness across multiple sections
- 12 sections have incomplete hints missing property names from attribute tables
- Pattern: Event handler properties (onchange, onselect, onfocus, onblur, onsubmit, onreset) are systematically missing
- Impact: Reduced searchability for these attributes

## Key Findings

The main issue is **hints completeness** (V4). Many tag sections are missing event handler and other property names in their hints arrays, even though these properties appear in the attribute tables. For optimal AI searchability, all property names from the first column of each attribute table should be included in hints.

Examples:
- `form-tag` missing: onsubmit, onreset, accept, acceptCharset, target
- Input tags missing: onselect, onchange
- Select/code tags missing: tabindex, onfocus, onblur, onchange

The content sections themselves are accurate and complete - only the hint metadata needs enhancement.

findings.description: Hints are missing property names from the attribute table: onsubmit, onreset, accept, acceptCharset, target. All property names from tables should be included in hints for searchability.
findings.source_evidence: Source table first column contains: name, action, method, enctype, onsubmit, onreset, accept, acceptCharset, target, autocomplete, windowScopePrefixes, useToken, secure, preventPostResubmit

findings.location: Section: text-tag (and similar input tags: search-tag, tel-tag, url-tag, email-tag, date-tag, month-tag, week-tag, time-tag, datetime-local-tag, number-tag, range-tag, color-tag)
